title: The Macro Minute: What matters more—tariff negotiations or Treasury auctions?

Happy Tuesday out there team 42. It's
your skipper here Darius Dell to present
our macro minute for Tuesday July 8th
2025. As always we'll start with the
executive summary from today's lit up
morning note. If you would like to
review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk
you need to be a client of 42 macro. So
today's macro question is what matters
more tariff negotiations or treasury
auctions? Our answer. Yesterday,
President Trump threatened sweeping 25%
tariffs on goods from a collection of
trading partners via social media with
implementation delayed until August 1st
as he left the door open to further
negotiation. Trump's Monday barrage
extended to a dozen other countries
including Japan, South Korea, South
Africa, Indonesia, Thailand, Cambodia
with and Cambodia with similar 25%
tariff threats posted in succession.
With the blood of any economic downturn
and budget deficit blowout now firmly on
the hands of his administration,
investors should continue to expect
trade negotiations to end well, even if
the uncertainty persists for a few more
months. To this point, President Trump
also signaled the August 1 deadline is
not 100% firm. He also softened his
stance slightly, saying, "We're not
proud. We're not going to be unfair."
And indicated a willingness to reward
countries that quote unquote offer
additional concessions. Elsewhere,
global long bonds are under pressure as
traditional buyers retreat amid rising
supply ahead of this week's 10-year and
30-year Treasury note and bond auctions.
The Eiguda Life Insurance Company, a key
longduration buyer, said it will avoid
Japanese long bonds for one to two years
due to fears of much higher rates amid
rising supply and BOJ policy
normalization. If Japanese insurers
won't buy JGBs, why would they buy bonds
from the US Treasury? Recall that both
Japanese and European investors are
guaranteed losses relative to
capitalizing their domestic bond markets
when factoring in the cost of hedging
what we believe is an ongoing secular
bare market in the US dollar. All
toaded, KISS, our systematic kiss
construction process provides main
street investors with the riskmanaged
solution to the massive problem Wall
Street will be forced to figure out the
hard way over the next half decade that
there is a geopolitically driven supply
demand imbalance in the Treasury bond
market that will likely require durable
Fed yield curve control to fix.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard
as always wrap up with a question from
our community. This one's called lack of
non- US equity exposure in KIS
portfolio. says, "Hello, uh, this is my
first week before Dacro. I'm going
through all the materials to get myself
acquainted with Kiss. Dr. Moams, my

why the lack of conviction in non US uh
equities." Uh, so there's no lack of
conviction in non US equities. There's
just I want to simplify KISS as much as
possible to make it as easy as possible
for you, the client, to remain on the
right side of marketplace for a very
long time so that you the client can
retire on time and comfortably. If you
want to make additional choices on top
of the stock standard KISS portfolio,
which features the S&P 500, gold, and
Bitcoin, be my guest. Like I I don't
understand why I keep getting questions
about, you know, this not being in the
standard Kiss portfolio, that not being
in the standard Kiss portfolio. Dr. MO
generates proper trade recommendations
for 70 factors, 70 independent factors
across global financial markets. If you
want to add international investments to
your KISS portfolio, then just use the
information that we're providing via Dr.
Mo to manage the exposures in your KISS
portfolio. Again, it's never I we we
could not be making it easier for
ordinary investors on Main Street to
stay on the right side of market risk
with the tools that the best investors
on Wall Street are using to do so to
accomplish the same exact goal. So, uh
for example, let's take you to the
global equities uh tab. So, uh this is
all MSEI all country world index X
United States. That's got a a long a max

recommendation. So if it has a maximum
position proper trade recommendation,
you know that if you're using this to
manage the equity exposure in your
customized KISS portfolio, you would
then just have a 60% position uh in uh
in ACWX. Uh if it was a long half
position, you'd have a 30% position
there. And if you had a no position,
then you would have a 0% position. So
again, it's it's so incredibly easy to

our KISS portfolio that is suitable for
your strategic investment objectives and
risk tolerance. You know, we have a lot
of clients who are, you know, 60 plus 70
plus uh uh that are managing their

construction process. Some of them have
elected to have more fixed income
exposure in their KISS process. Uh some

Bitcoin, so they don't have Bitcoin.
Maybe they have more stocks or more
bonds. I don't know that those are
choices that they have to make as
independent, thoughtful, do-it-yourself
investors. What we're providing, what we
sell here at 42 Macro are institutional
grade risk management overlays. We are
giving you hedge fund, not hedge fund
quality risk management. We're giving
you topflight hedge fund quality risk
management to add to your portfolio. And
we're giving be providing you with all
the tools that you need uh on a daily
basis, six days a week here uh to to to
implement to implement that and and
achieve your strategic investment
objectives. So uh you know again KISS is
S&P, gold and Bitcoin because that's the
simplest most effective way that we
found it's the best combination of
simple and effective. We can add
international we can add Ethereum or
Salana. We can add uh silver silver
miners, gold miners. We can make this
thing incredibly complicated, but it's
not going to make the results any
better. We want to simplify it as much

possible chance that you to retire on

within the US equity market or

thoughtful dispassionate, data driven,

you know, to to to satisfy your own uh
investment objectives. Again, what we're
doing is adding topflight hedge fund
quality risk management on top of your
portfolio. That's what we sell here at
42 macro. And then everything else we
say beyond that is just us helping you
try to anticipate how those risk
management overlays are going to evolve
so that when they do evolve, you feel
comfortable enough to dispassionately
execute the changes in your portfolio.
